EXT. THE GREAT WALL - NIGHT (SEQUENCE 1.0)

A MASSIVE, magnificent stone barrier snakes along the mountain tops of China. A lone GUARD patrols it. All's quiet, til ... WHOOSH! A falcon swoops over him, knocking off his helmet. It flies upward, RIPPING the Imperial flag from a pole; the crescent moon shines. The guard follows as the bird drops the flag. It flutters over the wall, revealing A DARK, OMINOUS MAN in SHADOW, clinging to the side. He GRABS the Guard before he can scream - LIFTING him into the air as HUNDREDS of grappling hooks clamp onto the wall. Startled guards look up as the shadowy MAN enters their sanctuary. As he RAISES his distinctive, curved sword ...
EMPEROR'S PALACE - NIGHT
Doors burst open. men quickly enter
The EMPEROR, his aide CHI FU, and several
A CAVERNOUS WAR ROOM - a huge model of China in its center. Your report, General. The General steps through a section of 'The Great Wall.'
(shocked) Impossible! No one can get through the Great Wall.
The Emperor holds up his hand.
Sharp intakes. This is no ordinary invader.
The General strides across the model with his pointer.
The Emperor slams the pointer.
(sternly) I'll not let him take the lives of any more of my people. The General nods respectfully.
Chi Fu!
Chi Fu stands at attention.
Deliver conscription notices throughout all the provinces, call up reserves and as many new recruits as possible! Forgive me, Your Majesty, but I believe our present force will be sufficient to
stop him.
I won't take any chances, General. (then) A single grain of rice can tip the (MORE)
scale ... one man may be the difference between victory and defeat.
As he stare.s out at the pale crescent moon ...
MULAN'S BEDROOM - DAY (SEQUENCE 1.9A)
CLOSE ON a bowl of rice. A pair of chopsticks reach for a mouthful. PAN UP to reveal MULAN, a high-spirited girl of 17 reading a scroll and copying notes onto her arm.
(reading) ... Quiet and demure. . . graceful ... . . . polite ... ( taking a bite) ... umph. . . deligate ... (mouth full) ... refined ... poised ...
She dips her brush in ink and adds one last thing.
... always on time!
A ROOSTER CROWS
MULAN' S BEDROOM
Mulan looks out the window and quickly gets up. MULAN She rushes out into
MAIN ROOM
Mulan looks around. Little brother! She runs in another direction, blowing on her arm.
She stops as she spots her dog lying on the ground.
On an adorable little, stupid dog.
Who's the smartest doggie in the world? C' mon, smart boy ... The dog pants excitedly. Mulan picks up a bag of grain.

Ready to do my chores? Tying the bag to his tail and a chicken leg over his head, she opens the door . As the dog chases the chicken leg into the yard, the grain bag breaks, sending the chickens into a feeding frenzy. Mulan watches -- i t ' l l do -- and quickly starts to gather teacups.
FA FAMILY TEMPLE
The hungry dog pursues the leg, running towards the TEMPLE.
FA FAMILY TEMPLE
FA ZHOU, Mulan's dignified father, kneels before the ancestral stone beis and lights incense.
The dog runs in; seconds later the chickens enter, pecking at the spilled grain.
FA FAMILY HOUSE
Mulan hurries to the temple, stopping to feed her dog. Fa Zhou slowly rises from the floor with the help of his car1e. He turns to see his beloved, tardy daughter.
(sweetly; setting out the tea) Well, first I have to meet someone special for tea.
She smiles at him; Fa Zhou tries not to smile, but can't help himself. She's got him.
ON ONE RED TEACUP among GREEN ONES. Mulan's hand reaches in. MULAN
Mulan spins the tray -- as it circles around, they each grab a cup. Fa Zhou holds up the red one. How do you always do that? Fa Zhou smiles and CHUCKLES, then stops the tray from spinning.
Mulan looks up at him -- she wants to please him.
(small smile) I won't let you down, Baba.
Full of hope, they sip their tea.
FRONT GATE - SHORTLY AFTER
Mulan comes charging out on KAHN, a magnificent black horse.
Let's go, Kahn!
She waves at Fa Zhou before leaping over the wall.
Wish me luck!
Fa Zhou shakes his head. Where does she get it from.
EXT. VILLAGE ROAD - CONTINUOUS
GRANDMOTHER FA, a tiny feisty woman, surveys a busy street filled with carts and travellers. She holds up a cage containing a CRICKE'r.
Okay, cricket, let's see how good you are.
With her eyes closed, she steps into traffic. All around her, vehicles SWERVE and COLLIDE to avoid her. Miracuously, she makes it across. Opening her eyes, she looks at the cricket gleefully.
You're a lucky one!
The cricket faints. FA LI, Mulan's mother, joins her.
(worried) Where's Mulan?
She points to a group of elegantly dressed girls.
The other girls have been preparing all morning!
There was an accident. She's probably stuck in traffic.
SFX: THUNDERING HOOVES. the mayhem trots A cloud of dust. Then, from within
elegantly seated on Kahn, side saddle, holding a parasol. She's oblivious to the leaves and brush entangled in her hair.
(GROANING; eyes rolling)
Mulan approaches her mother.

segment-speaker: MULAN But Mama, I had to-FA LI
segment-text: None of your excuses. You can't be late for the Matchmaker. First impressions are very important.
They lead her into ...
BATH SHOP (SEQUENCE 1.9B)
The BATHER looks Mulan over, and prepares her for a bath.
WELL HONEY I'VE SEEN WORSE WE'RE GOING TO TURN THIS SOW'S EAR INTO A SILK PURSE Mulan sits in the tub, shivering. MULAN It's freezing!
It would've been warm if you were here on time.
SPLASH! The Bather dumps water onto Mulan, then begins scrubbing her hair.
WE'LL HAVE YOU WASHED AND DRIED
TRUST MY RECIPE FOR INSTANT BRIDE YOU'LL BRING HONOR TO US ALL As Fa Li starts to wash Mulan's arm, she sees the crib notes.
Mulan, what's this? MULAN
Uh, notes ... in case I forget something? Grandma Fa slaps her head and hands the cricket to Fa Li.
Hold this. (turning away) We'll need more luck than I thought.
HAIRDRESSER'S SHOP
ON MULAN'S contorted face as TWO HAIRDRESSERS pull and style her hair.
WAIT AND SEE
VILLAGE STREE'l'
Mulan leaves the Hairdresser's shop. next stop, women smile at her. All: {Group of As she heads for her A GIRL CAN BRING HER FAMILY GREAT HONOR IN ONE WAY BY STRIKING A GOOD MATCH
DRESSMAKER' S SHOP
TWO DRESSMAKERS efficiently wrap Mulan in her gown. MEN WANT GIRLS WITH GOOD TASTE
extremely tight) Mulan: gasp
All: (Dressmaker #1 &: #2 and Fa Li)
VILLAGE STREET

Mulan and Fa Li continue to their next stop. Mulan watches as TWO LITTLE BOYS play war and TWO LITTLE GIRLS play with dolls. All: (Group of approx. 15-20 WHO GUARDS US FROM THE HUNS A MAN BY BEARING ARMS A GIRL BY BEARING SONS
women)
They stop at the fan stall. Fa Li tries to teach Mulan how to hold a fan gracefully, only it flies out of Mulan's hands.
HERE'S WHAT MEN CAN'T RESIST HOLD YOUR FAN AND GIVE YOUR WRIST A TWIST An Unlucky Passerby: {OWW) Fa Li: NEXT TIME TRY IT WITH A BIT LESS WRIST YOU'LL BRING HONOR TO US ALL
EXT. VILLAGE MARKETPLACE
Grandmother Fa inspects goods at a stall. She touches an old stone statue. The head falls off. She quickly replaces it with a little doll's head. From out of the shadows steps a creepy MERCHANT.
Ahhh, Grandma Fa... In what might I interest you? Tea, perfume ...
He looks around, furtively, then holds something up.
Rent the monkey?
The monkey stares at her with a blank smile. Grandmother Fa picks up a jade necklace.
I want these. They look lucky.
The merchant puts the monkey in her face .
The monkey is lucky. Grandmother Fa holds up the necklace.
I want the necklace!
The merchant swings the monkey around his neck.
The monkey is a necklace!
(picking her teeth with her knife) How 'bout I make you into a necklace?
MAKE-UP ARTIST'S SHOP TWO MAKE-UP WOMEN paint Mulan's face. Make-up Artist
(when we're through you can't fail) (when we're through you can't fail) (like a lotus blossom soft and pale)
(like a lotus blossom soft and pale)

She holds a mirror up. Mulan is the perfect porcelain vision, like all the other girls, except ... she takes a tiny piece of hair and curls it over her forehead. That's better. Fa Li unwraps a cloth, revealing a beautiful hair comb. Lovingly, she places it in Mulan's hair.
There, you're ready.
Mulan and her mother share a warm smile as Grandmother Fa enters, her arms full of various good luck charms.
She waddles over and stuffs an apple into Mulan's mouth.
(pinning) A pendant for balance ...
She places the jade necklace around Mulan's neck.
YOU MUST PROUDLY SHOW IT
NOW ADD A CRICKET JUST FOR LUCK
Grandma Fa ties the cricket to Mulan's waist.
EXT. VILLAGE PLACE
Fa Li and Grandmother Fa proudly watch as Mulan heads off to meet the Matchmaker.
ANCESTORS HEAR MY PLEA
KEEP MY FATHER STANDING TALL Mulan joins a row of girls approaching the Matchmaker's room. Young Girls: (4 + Mulan) SCARIER THAN THE UNDERTAKER

PLEASE LOOK KINDLY ON THESE CULTURED PEARLS EACH A PERFECT PORCELAIN DOLL PLEASE BRING HONOR TO US PLEASE BRING HONOR TO US PLEASE BRING HONOR TO US The girls bow down with parasols, waiting ... THE MATCHMAKER -
( SEQUENCE 1. 9C)
steps outside, looks at her notepad and calls out.
Mulan eagerly looks up and waves.
(writing; displeased) Speaking without permission ... MULAN
Mulan steps inside as her mother and grandmother look after her apprehensively.
( re : Matchmaker) Who spit in her bean curd.
INT. MATCHMAKER' S ROOM
Mulan stands silently as the Matchmaker circles her, inspecting.
Too skinny ... hmmf. Not good for bearing sons.

As she makes note, the cricket escapes and jumps onto the Matchmaker's shoulder. Mulan grabs him; tosses him. He hops back onto Mulan's shoulder, her head-- he won't leave. As the Matchmaker turns to face Mulan, she pops the cricket into her mouth.
Recite the final admonition. Mulan is caught.
(mouth full) Mrmnm .** bmmm.
Well?
A pause, then Mulan opens her fan and ...
(coughing; spitting) Ptoo!
..... spits out the cricket. Now, to impress her.
Fulfill your duties calmly and ... Needing help, she pulls up her sleeve. MULAN
snack-- act. The Matchmaker circles her, listening.
(quickly) This shall bring you honor and glory.
She breathes a sigh of relief. Not so fast. snatches the fan away, looking it over.
(suspiciously) Hmmm **.
Finding nothing, she grabs Mulan's arm and leads her off.
This way!
She points to a table set with tea; her hand covered with black ink from Mulan's arm.
Now, pour the tea. Mulan and the Matchmaker sit.
To please your future in-laws, you must demonstrate a sense of dignity ...
She rubs her fingers on her face, leaving an ink moustache. Mulan stares, and accidentally pours tea onto the table.
(eyes closed; grandly) ... and refinement.
Mulan sees the cricket relaxing in the tea cup. She panics.
You must also be poised.
The Matchmaker takes the cup.
Umm. . . pardon me.
... and silent! !
Mulan's stuck. She watches as the cricket's antennae curl up towards the Matchmaker's nose on each inhale.
(trying to remain calm} Ummm ... uh ... could I just-(reaching for the cup) ... take that back-- for a moment ...
She tugs at the cup. SPLASH! The tea spills on the Matchmaker as the cricket leaps down her dress.
Why you clumsy ... (noticing the cricket) Whaa ... Ooh! Aaagh! Oooh!
The Matchmaker knocks over and lands in the incense
(running around) Wah-ahhhh!
Mulan, trying to help, fans the Matchmaker's rear with her fan.
Her clothes catch fire.
Whooagghh!
EXT. VILLAGE PLACE
Fa Li, Grandmother Fa and the villagers listen to hysterical screams and crashing sounds. Mulan bursts outside, holding the tea pot. follows, still on fire.
Put it out! Put it out!
Put it ow-- SPLASH! Mulan throws tea in the Matchmaker's face, dousing the flames. The cricket hops out. little cage. Frightened, he jumps back into his The Matchmaker, steaming, turns to Mulan.
You may look like a bride, but you will never bring your family honor!
Fa Li and Grandma gather around Mulan consolingly. villagers bow their heads and start to turn away.
Her poor family ... what a shame ...
Mulan listens to the whispers.
EXT. MULAN' S HOUSE - LATER ( SEQUENCE 3 . 9)
Mulan solemnly leads Kahn through the front gate. She sees Fa Zhou smiling hopefully at her. She can't break his heart. Ducking behind Kahn, she walks off as her father watches.
EXT. STABLE
Kahn drinks from a trough. Mulan stands beside him, looking at her reflection in the water. REFLECTION SONG

LOOK AT ME I WILL NEVER PASS FOR A PERFECT BRIDE OR A PERFECT DAUGHTER CAN IT BE I'M NOT MEANT TO PLAY THIS PART NOW I SEE I WOULD BREAK MY FAM' LY' S HEART Throughout, Mulan sees her image in the garden pond, in the family temple ...
WHO IS THA'r GIRL I SEE S'rARING STRAIGHT
WHY IS MY REFLECTION SOMEONE I DON'T KNOW SOMEHOW I CANNOT HIDE WHO I AM THOUGH I'VE TRIED WHEN WILL MY REFLECTION SHOW WHO I AM INSIDE WHEN WILL MY REFLECTION SHOW WHO I AM INSIDE
FA FAMILY GARDEN
Mulan sits quietly under a blossoming tree, trying to fight back tears.
Mulan looks up, then hangs her head. Fa Zhou sits beside her, taking in the beauty of the trees.
My my ... what beautiful blossoms we have
this year. But look ... He points to an unopened blossom.
This one's late. But I ' l l bet that when it blooms ...
He takes Mulan's haircomb from her hands and gently puts it in her hair.
... It will be the most beautiful of all.
Mulan can't help but smile. LARGE DRUMS IN 'rHE DISTANCE Fa Zhou rises. MULAN
VILLAGE - MOMENTS LATER (SEQUENCE 4)
A VILLAGER BANGS a huge drum as SOLDIERS ON HORSES gallop into town.
INSIDE THE FA GATE
Fa Zhou and Fa Li peek into the street. Mulan tries to see.
Mulan, stay inside.
Grandmother Fa motions towards the wall, "you' 11 get a better view from there. " Mulan quickly climbs it to see VILLAGERS gathered around the soldiers.
Citizens! I bring a proclamation from the Imperial City! The Huns have invaded China!
Mulan reacts, shocked. The crowd gasps in horror as panic ensues.
The Huns!
Chi Fu reads the Emperor's decree.
By order of the Emperor, one man from every family must serve in the Imperial Army. (calling) The Hsiao family!
A middle-aged man steps forward to take his notice from a
The Yi family!
An OLD MAN steps forward, accompanied by his tall, healthy SON.
I will serve the Emperor in my father's place. The son respectfully takes the conscription notice as Chi Fu reads the next name.
The Fa family! MULAN (softly)
The crowd murmurs. Fa Zhou hands his cane to his wife. Gathering his strength, he steadily walks forward.
I am ready to serve the Emperor.
As he prepares to take the notice from the soldier ... Mulan RUNS OUT into the street.
Father, you can't go!
Mulan ... MULAN (addressing Chi Fu directly) Please, Sir, my father has already fought bravely for the Emperor.
Chi Fu points at her.
Silence! (to Fa Zhou) You would do well to teach your daughter to hold her tongue in a man's presence.
Fa Zhou looks ashamed.
Mulan, you dishonor me.
Mulan lowers her head.
Grandmother Fa starts to lead Mulan
(to Fa Zhou) Report tomorrow to the Wu Zhong camp.
Yes, s.ir.
Fa Zhou walks proudly past Fa Li, refusing to take his cane.
(calling more names) The Chu family! The Wen family! Chan family!
The
As Mulan, Fa Li and Grandma Fa quietly follow him ...
INT. FA ZHOU'S BEDROOM - LATER THAT DAY (SEQUENCE 5)

CLOSE ON a cabinet opening, revealing Fa Zhou's magnificent armor and sword. Mulan stands in the hallway, holding a candle. She watches as Fa Zhou picks up his sword and gracefully manuveurs it through a series of moves. He handles it well until PAIN SHOOTS through his leg.
Aagh!
CLANG! The sword drops to the ground. Grabbing his leg, Fa Zhou slowly stands against the wall, clutching his conscription notice.
IN THE HALLWAY
Mulan presses against the wall, anguished. the fate of her father. She hurries away. She's just seen
KITCHEN - LATER THAT NIGHT
Mulan, Fa Zhou, Fa Li and Grandma silently eat, ignoring the fact that this could be their last night together. Finally, Mulan is unable to contain herself. MULAN Why isn't anyone saying anything?
Mulan ... MULAN (standing; to Fa Zhou) You shouldn't have to go. There are plenty of young men to fight for China!
It is an honor to protect my country, and my family.
So you'll die for honor?
(voice rising) I will die doing what's right! MULAN
I know my place! (then) It is time you learned yours.
Mulan, hurt, runs out of the room. A moment before Fa Zhou slowly sits, and resumes eating. OUT ON THE PORCH Mulan leans against the post.
(BREAKING DOWN)
EXT. GARDEN - LATER THA'r NIGHT - ( SEQUENCE 6 )

RAIN beats down on the Great Stone Dragon, a magnificent statue. Mulan, lost in thought, sits at the base, staring at her reflection in a puddle. A LIGHTNING FLASH streaks across the sky; A CLAP OF THUNDER roars. Mulan is shaken from her reverie. She looks across the garden at her parents' bedroom window. MULAN'S POV Her parents silhouetted in the window. Fa Zhou tries to console Fa Li, only she tears away from him. A beat before Fa Zhou blows out a candle, sending the room into darkness. MULAN takes this in as the rain spills down her face. just sit by ... She makes her decision. She can't
INT. FA FAMILY TEMPLE
Mulan enters; she lights a stick of incense and places it in a little dragon incense holder. The cricket watches as she says a prayer to her ancestors, then hurries out. He follows her. INT.
FA ZHOU'S BEDROOM
Hard rain, lightning, thunder. Mulan approaches her sleeping parents and replaces the conscription notice on her father's nightstand with her hair comb. She looks at him tenderly, then leaves.
INT. FRONT ROOM

Mu1an removes her father's sword from its sheath and stretches out her long hair; she pauses for a moment-then SLASH! She CUTS her hair; it spills to the floor. She ties it up, then goes to her father's closet, staring in awe at his armor. She puts it on and holds his sword.
INT. STABLES

A shadow falls over Kahn. He looks up and WHINNIES in alarm. KAHN'S POV A warrior stands in the shadows. The figure moves forward, revealing Mulan dressed in her father's armor. Kahn stamps the ground. Mulan comforts him.
EXT. COURTYARD GATE

The Cricket watches as Mulan quietly walks Kahn out. She turns and takes one last look at her house. Seconds later, Mulan and Kahn BURST through the FRONT GATE. Lightning and thunder. The wind SLAMS the gate shut behind them. As they GALLOP into the night ...
FA FAMILY TEMPLE
CLOSE ON the frightening face of an ancestral guardian statue.
INT. GRANDMOTHER FA'S BEDROOM
Grandmother Fa bolts up in her bed, her eyes wide with terror. Another peal of THUNDER.
INT. FA ZHOU'S BEDROOM
Grandmother Fa bursts into the room.
Mulan is gone!
Fa Zhou sits up.
What?
He sees her haircomb on his nightstand, and slowly picks it up.
It can't be ...
He runs to his closet-- his armor is gone. LIGHTNING, ~'HUNDER.
EXT. COURTYARD
HARD RAIN. Fa Zhou runs into the courtyard still clutching the hair comb.
(painful wailing) MULAN!
He stumbles and falls, then sees something.
No ...
The front gate is BANGING open and shut. Fa Li rushes to her husband's side.
You must go after her -- she could be killed!
(solemnly) If I reveal her, she will be.
A CLAP OF THUNDER. Grandmother Fa looks imploringly towards
(pleading} Ancestors, hear our prayer. Mulan.
A FLASH OF LIGHTENING FILLS THE SKY.
INT" TEMPLE - CONTINUOUS - {SEQUENCE 7)

The last wisps of incense burn out as the WIND GUSTS" The Chinese characters on a great stone bei GLOW hot red. Moments later, A GHOST materializes on top of the bei. This is THE GHOST OF THE FIRST ANCESTOR. He peers at the little dragon incense burner and speaks with a deep, imposing voice that echoes through the temple.

Mushu. . . awaken ! Close on the dragon incense burner - trembling as his metal body transforms into a live dragon, and CRASHES to the ground. This is Mushu, a mini-sized dragon with a deep drive to prove he's as good as any guardian. What he lacks in magical powers, he makes up in charm. He rises out of the incense smoke.
I live! (then} So tell me what mortal needs my protection, Great Ancestor. You just say the word and I'm there!
Mushu ...
And lemme say something foolish enough to threaten our family, vengeance will be mine!!!
He begins growling and stalking the room, monster-like"
Mushu!
Mushu stops cold. He looks up to see the First Ancestor pointing to a row of stone animals atop pedestals.
(to Mushu) These are the family guardians. (like a teacher) They ... ?
(obviously) protect the family.
And you, oh demoted one?
(put in his place) I ... ring the gong.
That's right. (parent to child) Now wake up the ancestors.
(sighing) One family reunion coming right up.
He leaps in front of the crowd of ancestral tablets.
(banging his gong) Okay, people, people! Look alive! Let's go, c'mon, get up! Let's move it! Rise and shine! You're all way past the beauty sleep thing! Trust me!
The room fills with THE GHOSTS OF THE FA ANCESTORS rising from the tablets lining both sides of the temple.
I knew it! I knew it! That Mulan was a troublemaker from the start.
Don't look at me - she gets it from your side of the family.
She's just trying to help her father. Ancestor 6 tallies on an abacus.
But if she's discovered ... (clicking the beads) Fa Zhou will be forever shamed, dishonor will come to the family, traditional values will disintegrate ...
Ancestors 7 and 8 stand in an "American Gothic" pose.
Not to mention they'll lose the farm!
Mushu sits back, reading the paper.
My children never caused such trouble, they all became acupuncturists.
Well, we can't all be acupuncturists.
No - Your great granddaughter had to be a cross-dresser! ! An imposing male ancestor points to the row of stone animals.
Let a guardian bring her back!
An excited murmur sweeps through the room. Ancestor 3 grabs Mushu and his gong and points to a stone animal.
Awaken the most cunning ... ANCESTOR 6 (MALE) (grabbing Mushu) The swiftest ...
(grabbing Mushu) No, send the wisest!
Silence! The ancestors grow still.
We must send the most powerful of all! (laughing) Okay, okay, I get the drift.
Mushu stands proudly on his pedestal.
The ancestors burst into derisive laughter.
You all don't think I can do it? this here.
He blows a tiny little flame from his mouth. OWW!
Jump back -- I'm pretty hot, huh? FIRST ANCESTOR (admonishing) You had your chance to protect the Fa family. Your misguidance led Fa Deng to disaster.
FA DENG, an ancestor holding his head in his hands, glares at
Yeah, thanks a lot.
(staring blankly) And your point is ...
The First Ancestor appears beside Mushu, floating in mid-air.
The point is we will be sending a real dragon to retrieve Mulan.

What-- what-- who you calling-- I'm a real dragon! You are not worthy of this spot ... (leaning in; sneering) and you never will be. (MORE) FIRST ANCESTOR (cont'd) (snarls) Now awaken the Great Stone Dragon!
He throws Mushu out the door.
A beat before Mushu pokes his
(smiling) So, you'll get back to me on the job thing.
A GONG FLIES through the air, hitting Mushu in the face. The First Ancestor rubs his temple and sighs.
EXT. COURTYARD
Mushu starts down to the Great Stone Dragon.
( frustrated) Just one chance; is that too much to ask? I mean, it's not like it'd kill you!
Mushu walks up to the enormous Stone Dragon perched on a crumbling pedestal and starts BANGING his gong.
Yo, Rocky! Wake up! You've got to go fetch Mulan! (boy to dog) C'mon, boy! Go get her! Go on!
Mushu throws his mallet over his shoulder and starts to climb
(GROWLING) No response.
Mushu hits the Dragon's ear with his gong.
Hello. . . hellooo ! ! ! (off huge whack) Hello!!!
The Dragon"s ear comes loose in his hand.
Uh-oh.

He tries to stick the ear back on, but it's hopeless. The Great Stone Dragon CRUMBLES, taking Mushu down with it. As the dust clears, Mushu COUGHS and peers at the Great Stone Dragon's face, the only piece intact.
Mushu freezes, then sees The First Ancestor, at the window of the family temple.
Have you awakened?

A beat before the head of the Great Stone Dragon pops up from the bushes. Mushu struggles to hold it steady. Then, he lowers his voice and assumes a persona, a la the Great and Powerful Oz. He waggles the stone face as he speaks.
(god-like) I, ah, I ah, yes I just woke up! I am the Great Stone Dragon. Good morning! I will go forth and fetch Mulan! ... Did I mention that I was the Great Stone Dragon?
Go! The fate of the Fa family rests in your claws!
(as the Great Stone Dragon) Don' t even worry about it! I will not lose face!
He loses his grasp on the face. Mushu still holding on. It rolls down the hill,
(falling) Ahhh oww, oh ohh owwww ...
SMASH! He lands beside the Great Stone Dragon's pedestal; the head on top of him.
(in pain) OWWW, my elbow. Ohh ...
He kicks off the head and sits up dejectedly.
That's just great-- now what?! I'm doomed! And all 'cause Miss Man decided to take her little drag show on the road!
Cri-Kee, the cricket, tugs on Mushu, CHIRPING, "Why don't you go get her?" Mushu looks down at him.
Whaddya mean, go get her? Are you crazy? The Ancestors don't want me near her. {then, picking up a piece of
After this mess, I'd have to bring her home with a medal to get back in there.
Mushu stares at the temple, thinks a beat, then smiles.
The CRICKET NODS and starts to take off as Mushu follows.
Mushu runs past him. Cri-Kee panics, watching Mushu exit towards the gate. Cri-Kee CHIRPS, "I'm Lucky." Mushu keeps running.
The cricket CHIRPS some more as he catches up to Mushu.
And as they take off down the road ...
HUN CAMP - NIGHT (SEQUENCE 8.6)
The still night sky - a FALCON cuts across. Then, a shadowy figure gallops by on a horse ... Shan-Yu. SFX: THUNDERING HOOVES as a swarm of Huns closely follow.
night, until

They race through the CLOSE ON SHAN-YU. Sensing something, he quickly motions his army to be still. Reins pull, horses instantly quiet. He signals, sending his ELITE SQUAD of five into the brush. Seconds later, TWO CHINESE SOLDIERS are thrown at Shan-Yu's feet.
Imperial scouts.

Shan-Yu jumps off his horse, carrying a torch. SLOWLY PAN up to reveal his huge, threatening form for the first time. The men gasp. He drops the torch in front of them and approaches, the falcon alighting on his arm.
Nice work, gentlemen ... you found the hun army.
The Huns CHUCKLE. Shan-Yu moves in closer, and starts to fix SCOUT ONE'S scarf.
Wouldn't your superiors be proud. {scared) The Emperor will stop you.
(calmly} Stop me? He invited me.
He grabs Scout 2, lifting him high into the air.
(voice rising} By building his wall, he challenged my strength. Well, I'm here to play his game. (quietly; in his face) Who are you betting on?
The Scout sweats. Shan-Yu throws him to the ground.
Go! Tell your Emperor to send his strongest armies!
Shan-Yu watches them run off.
I'm ready. He thinks for a moment, then turns to the ARCHER HUN. SHAN-YU How many men does it take to deliver a
The Archer lines up an arrow on his bow.
One.
As he pulls the arrow back ...
EXT. HILLTOP - DAY -
